nodes.x: Then Job answered and said,
Then Job answered and said,
But Job answered and said,
And Job answered and said,
Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
¶ Then Job answered the Lord, and said,
I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.
Now what is this other Comforter? It is no more or less than tho Loud J HSUS Cuius-ir
Calling on the name of the Lord for the Comforter, which shall teach them all things that are expedient for them—
For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water, because the comforter that should relieve my soul is far from me: my children are desolate, because the enemy prevailed.
Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?
Remember how short my time is: wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?
And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.

by the inflections of verbs, nouns, and adjectives, may be estimated at over FORTY THOUSAND. This heterogeneous mass of words, as found in the English vocabulary, when drawn out in line of discourse according to the laws of syntax, and embellished by the force of rhetorical elocution, has made nations to tremble and empires to quake. More glorious conquests have been achieved and victories won by…
Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul’s stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.

And now, my brethren, I would that ye should hear me, for I speak in the energy of my soul; for behold, I have spoken unto you plainly that ye cannot err, or have spoken according to the commandments of God.
Behold, I speak unto you as though I spake from the dead; for I know that ye shall have my words.

And now my beloved brethren, I have spoken these words unto you according to the Spirit which testifieth in me; and my soul doth exceedingly rejoice, because of the exceeding diligence and heed which ye have given unto my word.
My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Therefore, let your soul be at rest concerning your spiritual standing, and resist no more my voice.
But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should assuage your grief.
My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.
Though I speak, my grief is not assuaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased?
With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.

And, behold, one like the similitude of the sons of men touched my lips: then I opened my mouth, and spake, and said unto him that stood before me, O my lord, by the vision my sorrows are turned upon me, and I have retained no strength.
Though I speak, my grief is not assuaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased?

It is a very difficult matter to say anything at a time of sorrow and bereavement like the present that will give immediate relief to the sorrowing hearts of those who mourn. Such griefs can only be fully relieved by the lapse of time and the influence of the good spirit
I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.

“Remove from you,” says he, “ grief ; for she 
is the sister of doubt and anger.” “ How, sir,” 
say I, “is she the sister of these? for anger, 
doubt, and grief seem to be quite different from 
each other.” “You are senseless, O man. Do 
you not perceive that grief is more wicked than 
all the spirits, and most terrible to the servants 
of God, and more than all other spirits destroys 
man and…
¶ Woe is me for my hurt! my wound is grievous: but I said, Truly this is a grief, and I must bear it.

1. “ Put away,” said he, “ grief from yourself, for 
this also is a sister of double-mindedness and _ bitter- 
ness.’ 2. “ How, sir,’ I said, “is she their sister, 
for it seems to me that bitterness is one thing and 
double-mindedness is another, and grief another?” 
“You are foolish, O man,’ he said, “and do not 
understand that grief is more evil than all the 
spirits, and is most terrible to…
But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.
Then I said, I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength for nought, and in vain: yet surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.
Then I said, I have labored in vain, I have spent my strength for naught and in vain; surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.
I will lay thy cities waste, and thou shalt be desolate, and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.
Thus saith the Lord God; When the whole earth rejoiceth, I will make thee desolate.
He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.
And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, which is a witness against me: and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.
Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.
Mine eyes fail for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?
Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.
Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
My flesh trembleth for fear of thee; and I am afraid of thy judgments.
He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
I know his wrath, saith the Lord; but it shall not be so; his lies shall not so effect it.
Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.
They gaped upon me with their mouths, as a ravening and a roaring lion.
And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.
With hypocritical mockers in feasts, they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
All they that see me laugh me to scorn: they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying,
God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.
Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
And it came to pass that the Spirit said unto me again: Slay him, for the Lord hath delivered him into thy hands;
And I will deliver thee out of the hand of the wicked, and I will redeem thee out of the hand of the terrible.
The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness: according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.
He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.
He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.
And it came to pass, when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled, and got him out.
And it came to pass, as I lifted up my voice and cried, that he left his garment with me, and fled out.
His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.
He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.
He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.
And he said unto his lad, Run, find out now the arrows which I shoot. And as the lad ran, he shot an arrow beyond him.
And ye shall compass the king round about, every man with his weapons in his hand: and he that cometh within the ranges, let him be slain: and be ye with the king as he goeth out and as he cometh in.
He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.
Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth: as he hath caused a blemish in a man, so shall it be done to him again.
For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.
He runneth upon him, even on his neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:
For, behold, the Lord commandeth, and he will smite the great house with breaches, and the little house with clefts.
He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and I make sackcloth their covering.
I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.

wood, and have not been able to get it. I have cut it down, and piled it up, and still have not got it. I wonder if anybody else can say so. Have any of you piled up your wood, and when you have gone back could not find it? Some stories could be told of this kind, that would make professional thieves ashamed. It is not all of this community that possesses such spirits. A flock of sheep consisting…
My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.
My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;
I am weary of my crying: my throat is dried: mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.
My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.

has been necessary to shed one tear about it. It always takes something besides chastisement, or afflictions heaped upon us by our enemies, to bring tears from me. I can cry for joy, I can cry on beholding my friends after being separated from them. The soft, loving, still, small voice of the Spirit will bring tears to my eyes, but all the sufferings that could be brought upon me by the malice of…
Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.
or imprisoned, or be burned, butchered, or hung. That our great, and free country may never be afflicted with such a species of despotism, is my most earnest prayer. Amen.
For my love they are my adversaries: but I give myself unto prayer.

And now behold, my beloved brethren, I say unto you, do not suppose that this is all; for after ye have done all these things, if ye turn away the needy, and the naked, and visit not the sick and afflicted, and impart of your substance, if ye have, to those who stand in need—I say unto you, if ye do not any of these things, behold, your prayer is vain, and availeth you nothing, and ye are as…
O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.
From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto thee.
Let my cry come near before thee, O Lord: give me understanding according to thy word.
Let my prayer come before thee: incline thine ear unto my cry;
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
Also now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my record is on high.
Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.
This Jesus hath God raised up, whereof we all are witnesses.
My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.
But mine eyes are unto thee, O God the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
Mine eyes fail for thy salvation, and for the word of thy righteousness.

And mine eye shall not spare, neither will I have pity: I will recompense thee according to thy ways and thine abominations that are in the midst of thee; and ye shall know that I am the Lord that smiteth.
O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour!
Like as I pleaded with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I plead with you, saith the Lord God.
The Lord standeth up to plead, and standeth to judge the people.
So he that goeth in to his neighbour’s wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.
But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?
When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.
Before I go whence I shall not return, even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;
Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.
The fierce anger of the Lord shall not return, until he have done it, and until he have performed the intents of his heart: in the latter days ye shall consider it.

and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh; So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors. Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all
Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled.
